perf(inference): add DenseAttention workspace + wire into BlockWorkspace (#3339)

## What Changed

Follow-up to #3338. Adds zero-allocation attention forward path:

- **\DenseAttentionWorkspace\**: holds Q/K/V projection buffers,
attn_out, scores, weights eliminates 7 Vec allocations per attention
call
- **\DenseAttention::forward_into(x, out, ws)\**: writes o_proj result
directly into caller's buffer
- **\BlockWorkspace\**: renamed \ fn_out\ \delta\ (shared buffer reused
by both attention and FFN sub-blocks); added attention workspace field
- **\ orward_into_ws()\**: now fully zero-alloc uses attention workspace
+ FFN workspace

### Impact

Eliminates **12 Vec allocations per transformer block per forward pass**
(7 attention + 5 FFN). For a 32-layer model: **384 fewer allocations per
inference**.

Combined with #3338, the entire dense forward pass (DenseModel::forward
blocks attention + FFN) now allocates only at the model level
(embedding, ping-pong buffers, workspace, lm_head output) zero per-layer
allocations.

## What I Ran Locally

\\\
cargo check -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu #
OK
cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- dense_forward # 25/25 pass
cargo clippy -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu --
-D warnings # clean
rustfmt crates/bitnet-inference/src/dense_forward.rs  # formatted
\\\

## CI Jobs Relied On

CI Core required gates (Build & Test, BDD Grid Check, Clippy,
Documentation, CI Core Success)

## Parity Evidence

- \ est_attention_forward_into_matches_forward\: attention forward_into
matches allocating forward (new)
- \ est_forward_into_ws_matches_forward_into\: full workspace path
matches non-workspace path
- \	est_model_forward_uses_workspace\: model-level determinism verified
- All 19 existing tests continue to pass (backward compat)

## Docs Touched

None (internal API addition).

## Risks

Low  builds on #3338 foundation. BlockWorkspace API changed (\
ew\ takes additional \&DenseAttentionConfig\ param, \ fn_out\ renamed to
\delta\). Only internal callers.

## Recommended Disposition

**A) MERGE NOW**

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

incomplete type (extern const uint8 zclApp_DateCode[]).
Replace all sizeof(zclApp_DateCode) with FW_VERSION_STAMP_LEN (17).

feat: integrate AddGame with GamesStore for unified mutations (#164) (#165)

# Pull Request

## Description

Integrate `AddGameUseCase` into `GamesStore` to unify all collection
mutations through a single store orchestrator. This refactoring removes
the direct use-case dependency from `GameForm` (making it a pure UI
component), aligns `addGame` with the established patterns for
`editGame` and `deleteGame`, and ensures the store cache stays
synchronized after adding games.

## Related Issue

Closes #164

## Type of Change

- ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- 🔨 Refactoring (code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a
feature)
- 📝 Documentation update
- 🧪 Test update

## Changes Made

- Update `AddGameUseCaseInterface` return type from `Result<void>` to
`Result<Game>`
- Modify `AddGameUseCase` to return the persisted `Game` entity on
success
- Add `addGame(dto: AddGameDTO): Promise<Result<Game,
ApplicationErrorInterface>>` method to `GamesStore`
- Inject `AddGameUseCaseInterface` into `GamesStore` constructor
- Update `serviceCollection.ts` to pass `AddGameUseCase` to `GamesStore`
- Refactor `GameForm` to accept `onSubmit` prop callback (removes direct
use-case dependency)
- Update `AddGame.tsx` page to wire the store and pass submission
handler to `GameForm`
- Add comprehensive test block for `addGame` in `GamesStore.test.ts`
- Update `AddGameUseCase.test.ts` assertions to verify returned `Game`
entity
- Refactor `GameForm.test.tsx` to mock `GamesStoreInterface` instead of
`AddGameUseCaseInterface`
- Simplify and update documentation in `docs/use-cases/add-game.md` and
`docs/layers/ui-layer.md`
- Update `DECISIONS.md` and `README.md` to reference all 11 ADRs and
recent design decisions

## Architecture Layer(s) Affected

- Application (use cases, ports/interfaces)
- Presentation (UI components, views, controllers)

## Testing

### Test Coverage

- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [x] Integration tests added/updated
- [x] Component tests added/updated (GameForm)
- [x] All existing tests pass

### Manual Testing

1. Navigate to the "Add Game" page via the UI
2. Fill in game details (title, platform, format, etc.)
3. Submit the form and verify the new game appears in the collection
list immediately
4. Confirm no page refresh is required
5. Navigate away and back to the collection home and verify the newly
added game persists
6. Test error handling by submitting invalid data and verifying error
messages display
7. Verify navigation to home page occurs on successful submission

## Performance Impact

No significant performance impact. Refactoring maintains the same
performance characteristics; store mutation flow is equivalent to
`editGame` and `deleteGame`.

## Breaking Changes

`AddGameUseCaseInterface.execute()` return type changed from
`Result<void>` to `Result<Game>`. This is an internal application-layer
change; external consumers route through `GamesStore.addGame()`, which
maintains the same API.

## Checklist

- [x] Code follows the project's Clean Architecture guidelines
- [x] Code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] Self-review of code performed
- [x] Comments added for complex logic
- [x] Documentation updated (use-cases, architecture, decisions)
- [x] No new warnings or errors introduced
- [x] All acceptance criteria from issue #164 met

## Additional Notes

**Alignment Verification:** ✅ All 5 acceptance criteria from issue #164
have been fully implemented:

1. ✅ `AddGameUseCase` returns `Result<Game>` with the persisted entity
2. ✅ `GamesStore` exposes `addGame(dto: AddGameDTO)` following
`editGame` pattern
3. ✅ `GameForm` delegates via `onSubmit` prop (no direct use-case
dependency)
4. ✅ `AddGame` page wires the store and handles navigation on success
5. ✅ All tests updated and passing

**Technical Details:**

- The `GamesStore.addGame()` implementation mirrors `editGame()`
exactly: delegates to use case, adds entry to map on success, commits
changes
- `GameForm` is now a pure presentation component with symmetric prop
APIs for add/edit modes
- Store auto-triggers fetch via `queueMicrotask` (per ADR-011), ensuring
downstream observers are notified of state changes
- E2E test simplified to reflect the new flow through the store

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): harden session creation input validation (#104)

## Summary
Adds type validation for `args` and `initialCommand` parameters in
session creation to address CodeQL alert #14 (code injection).

### Changes
- **routes.js**: Validate `args` is an array of strings and
`initialCommand` is a string at the API layer
- **sessions.js**: Defense-in-depth type checks before `pty.spawn()`
- **routes.test.js**: 3 new tests covering invalid `args` (non-array,
mixed types) and non-string `initialCommand`

### Context
CodeQL flags user-provided values flowing into `pty.spawn()` as code
injection. While this is by design (TermBeam is a terminal emulator),
adding type validation prevents unexpected object types from reaching
the PTY layer.

Closes #98

commit.message:
feat: parallelize eval harness with 4-server matrix strategy

Previous run timed out at 120 min (114/300 evals, ~63s avg sequential).
Now uses GitHub Actions matrix to run 4 servers in parallel:
- Each matrix job: 75 evals (25 scenarios × 3 models), 90 min timeout
- merge-and-report job: combines 4 partial results, scores, creates issue
- Wall-clock: ~80 min instead of ~315 min (4× speedup)

Changes:
- run_docs_eval.py: add --server flag for single-server mode
- eval_scorer.py: accept multiple input files, merge before scoring
- docs-eval-harness.yml: matrix strategy + merge-and-report job

Reject multi-layer OCI bundles and validate WASM content type (#231)

* Initial plan

* Reject multi-layer OCI bundles and validate WASM content type

Add `validate_single_wasm_layer()` that ensures OCI bundles contain
exactly one layer with `application/wasm` media type, per the WASM OCI
artifact spec. Called in both `Manager::pull()` and
`Manager::pull_with_progress()` before storing data.

Adds spec requirements `oci.layers.reject-multi` and
`oci.layers.require-wasm-content-type` with Tracey annotations.
